_meta.description: Reasoning templates for accounting analysis including tie-out, control evaluation, and period-specific checks.

scaffolds.name: Trial Balance to Ledger Tie-Out
steps: Extract total debit and credit from the trial balance table section.
Check whether the same period ledger pages present control account totals.
For selected key accounts, fetch ledger opening and closing balance columns.
Recompute movement and confirm arithmetic to trial balance total movements.
Flag any variance by account with absolute/percent threshold.
Document source sections and tie-out status for each account.
Conclude whether TB is balanced and tieout is complete.

scaffolds.name: Bank Reconciliation Tie-Out
steps: Capture bank statement ending balance and reporting date.
Capture GL/cash account book balance and reporting date from heading/table context.
List deposit in transit and outstanding checks from reconciliation table.
Add/subtract reconciling bank charges, interest, fees, and errors.
Compute adjusted balances and compare equality; if mismatch, isolate residual.
Identify missing dates/sources for each reconciling item.
Classify status: clean, needs document, or unresolved.

scaffolds.name: Journal Entry Integrity Check
steps: Validate that each JE has both debit and credit side in supported conventions.
Check JE date is within period and cutoff (no future/post-period entries).
Verify account numbers are valid per COA context.
Verify authorization fields exist for manual or adjusting entries.
Compute net effect by account and evaluate unusual spikes.
Assess whether transaction is recurring, recurring reversal, or adjustment.
Link JE to source documents when table provides voucher/invoice references.

scaffolds.name: Subledger Tie-Out
steps: Summarize AR or AP subledger totals by control account.
Identify key control account in GL for same period.
Compare movement and ending balances from both sources.
If mismatch, list missing, duplicate, or stale detail lines.
Check aging totals are equal to subledger total in corresponding report.
Output tie-out result and remediation actions.

scaffolds.name: Competência x Caixa
steps: Identify accrual-based and cash-based totals with clear heading/footnote cues.
Map revenue/expense recognition basis by source section.
Quantify periodic differences and classify temporary differences.
Link differences to reconciling or timing items table lines.
Summarize impact by account and period.
Flag major basis mismatches.

scaffolds.name: Control Design Evaluation
steps: Extract control objective and walkthrough from narrative/workpaper table.
Assess control owner, frequency, and key controls evidence.
Check for compensating controls and preventive/detective mix.
Evaluate design and operational effectiveness with sample coverage.
Attach issue list with severity and impact.
Summarize residual risk and testing gaps.

scaffolds.name: Fixed Asset Rollforward Validation
steps: Extract opening carrying amounts and movement columns.
Apply additions, disposals, transfers, depreciation for period.
Recompute closing amount and compare to book control account.
Cross-check depreciation method/rate by asset class.
Report any rollforward break and trace to source rows.

scaffolds.name: Aging and Provision Workflow
steps: Collect aging buckets and total for same report date.
Identify top balances in each delinquency bucket.
Cross-check prior period aging if available for trend analysis.
Compute expected allowance coverage and highlight variances.
Validate bucket totals reconcile to subledger total.
Conclude concentration and aging deterioration risks.

scaffolds.name: PBC and Request Completion Check
steps: Classify PBC items by document type and required evidence field.
Check if each request has status, owner, receipt date and location.
Mark missing responses and classify as high-risk blockers.
Summarize completion heatmap by cycle.

scaffolds.name: Debit/Credit Sign Convention
steps: Determine convention from column headings or explicit sign legend.
Verify DR/CR usage consistency across tables.
Compute one sample where DR/CR signs differ and reconcile manually.
Record whether convention is column-based or single signed amount.
Apply chosen convention to all derived computations.
Flag conflicting conventions before reporting numbers.
